fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Rhode Island Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Rhode Island - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity rhode island, how to transfer physical therapist license to rhode island, physical therapist compact rhode island, rhode island physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Rhode Island DOH — PT Licensure
board.mailing_address: Rhode Island Department of Health, Health Professions Regulation, 3 Capitol Hill, Room 104, Providence, RI 02908-5097

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Rhode Island.

steps.title: Create Account and Start Online Application
steps.description: Register an account on the RI Department of Health's Online Licensing Services Portal and initiate a new Physical Therapist application, carefully completing all sections.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts and NPTE Scores
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your accredited physical therapy program to be sent directly from the educational institution to the RI Department of Health. Also, ensure your National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) scores are sent directly from the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT).

steps.title: Provide Verification of Licensure (If Applicable)
steps.description: If you hold or have held a physical therapist license in another state, arrange for verification of licensure to be sent directly from each state's licensing board to the RI Department of Health.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: Undergo a state and national criminal background check, including fingerprinting, as mandated by the Rhode Island Department of Health. Follow the instructions provided during the application process to complete this step.

steps.title: Upload Supporting Documents and Pay Fee
steps.description: Upload any additional required documents, such as proof of identity, a passport-style photo, and an affidavit of good moral character, through the Online Licensing Services Portal. Pay the non-refundable $100 application fee.

steps.title: Application Review and Licensure
steps.description: The Rhode Island Board of Physical Therapy will review your complete application and all supporting documentation. Upon approval, your physical therapist license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Rhode Island physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Rhode Island have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers physical therapist practice in Rhode Island. To work in Rhode Island, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Rhode Island?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Rhode Island while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Rhode Island
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Rhode Island, you must submit your initial application and all required documentation through the RI Department of Health's Online Licensing Services Portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the RI Department of Health's Online Licensing Services Portal, click 'Apply Online' for Health Professionals, then select 'Physical Therapists' from the dropdown to start a new application. If you do not have an existing account, you will be prompted to create one by providing your email address and setting up a password.
